etymology.description: semantic 金(金) (jīn, "metal") + phonetic 艮(艮) (gèn) -> silver
etymology.interpretation: The character 銀(銀) (yín, "silver") combines 金(金) (jīn, "metal"), indicating that it is a type of metal, with the phonetic component 艮(艮) (gèn), which contributes the sound. This combination refers specifically to the metal silver.
etymology.simplification: 銷(金) was reduced to 钅(钅) to create a simpler form while retaining the phonetic 艮(艮).

examples.chinese: 银色在阳光下很耀眼(銀色在陽光下很耀眼)。
examples.pinyin: Yínsè zài yángguāng xià hěn yàoyǎn.
examples.english: Silver is very dazzling in the sunlight.

examples.chinese: 老奶奶给了我一个银戒指(老奶奶給了我一個銀戒指)。
examples.pinyin: Lǎo nǎinai gěi le wǒ yī gè yín jièzhǐ.
examples.english: The grandmother gave me a silver ring.

examples.chinese: 银色的月光洒在湖面(銀色的月光灑在湖面)。
examples.pinyin: Yínsè de yuèguāng sǎ zài húmiàn.
examples.english: The silver moonlight spreads over the lake.
